title: lupinus parviflorus var. fulvomaculatus
intro: Lupinus parviflorus var. fulvomaculatus, also known as the dwarf lupine or western lupine, is a perennial legume native to western North America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and skin irritations due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Lupinus parviflorus var. fulvomaculatus include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate skin conditions such as eczema and acne. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the affected areas.
